_description: Near-empty wallet (₹8,000 remaining) with planned cholecystectomy. Tests WALLET_INSUFFICIENT flag and copayment_required=True in Phase 5.

clinical.chief_complaints: Recurrent right upper quadrant colicky pain after fatty food for 8 months, 3 episodes of jaundice, one episode of acute cholecystitis last month
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient has been experiencing recurrent right upper quadrant pain for 8 months, triggered by fatty food. She has had 3 documented episodes of jaundice and one hospitalisation for acute cholecystitis last month which resolved with IV antibiotics. USG has confirmed multiple gallstones. Now presenting for elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy after interval.

examination_findings.general: Conscious, oriented, mildly icteric sclera, no pallor
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, mild tenderness in right hypochondrium, Murphy sign negative today, liver not enlarged, no palpable gallbladder

investigations.result_summary: Multiple calculi in gallbladder (largest 1.4cm), gallbladder wall thickened 4mm, no CBD dilatation, no pericholecystic fluid
LFT: Total bilirubin 1.8 mg/dL, SGOT 42 U/L, SGPT 58 U/L, ALP 142 U/L. Hb 10.8 g/dL, WBC 9400/cumm. PT-INR 1.1.

clinical.past_medical_history: Cholecystitis hospitalisation last month. Anaemia on iron supplementation.
clinical.past_surgical_history: Lower segment caesarean section 3 years ago
clinical.notes: Patient wallet nearly exhausted from prior claims this year (₹8,000 remaining). Co-payment discussion needed before proceeding. Patient has been counselled.
